careProfile.summary: The Labrador Retriever is a large-sized breed with active energy and heavy shedding.
careProfile.ownerFit: great with families and children; needs an active owner with time for daily exercise; highly trainable — ideal for first-time owners; expect regular grooming and vacuuming
careProfile.exerciseNeeds: Needs 90+ minutes of vigorous daily activity — fetch, running, or agility.
careProfile.trainingNotes: Highly trainable — responds well to positive reinforcement and consistency.
careProfile.groomingNotes: Heavy shedder — brush 3–4× per week; deshedding tools recommended seasonally.
careProfile.feedingContext: Feed 2 meals daily; adjust portions by activity level. High-energy breeds benefit from performance formulas.

costDrivers.insurance: ~$52/mo base (varies by age, state, and plan)
costDrivers.grooming: $15–$30/mo in grooming tools and occasional baths
costDrivers.training: Group puppy classes from $100–$200; optional private sessions $50–$150/hr
costDrivers.vetCare: Annual wellness visits $200–$400; breed-specific screening recommended
